sourceRefs.fallbackText: If check_circular is true (the default), then lists, dicts, and custom encoded objects will be checked for circular references during encoding to prevent an infinite recursion (which would cause a RecursionError ). Otherwise, no such check takes place.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: Unlike pickle and marshal , JSON is not a framed protocol, so trying to serialize multiple objects with repeated calls to dump() using the same fp will result in an invalid JSON file.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: If allow_nan is true (the default), then NaN , Infinity , and -Infinity will be encoded as such. This behavior is not JSON specification compliant, but is consistent with most JavaScript based encoders and decoders. Otherwise, it will be a ValueError to encode such floats.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: object_pairs_hook ( callable | None) – If set, a function that is called with the result of any JSON object literal decoded with an ordered list of pairs. The return value of this function will be used instead of the dict . This feature can be used to implement custom decoders. If object_hook is also set, object_pairs_hook takes priority. Default None .

sourceRefs.fallbackText: allow_nan ( bool ) – If False , serialization of out-of-range float values ( nan , inf , -inf ) will result in a ValueError , in strict compliance with the JSON specification. If True (the default), their JavaScript equivalents ( NaN , Infinity , -Infinity ) are used.
